sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Inter, currently positioned 2nd in the league with 74 points, host Roma, who sit in 5th place with 63 points, in Round 34 at the iconic San Siro/Giuseppe Meazza stadium in Milan. This matchup promises to be an intense battle as Inter look to leverage their strong home form against a resilient Roma side. The referee for this clash, Michael Fabbri, averages 4.8 yellow cards per game and 0.26 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially feisty encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Inter have dominated with 4 wins compared to Roma’s solitary victory. Their most recent meeting on October 20, 2024, saw Inter edge out a 1-0 win away at Roma, with Mile Svilar earning Player of the Match honors with a 7.8 Sofascore rating. Historically, Inter have been the first to score in 9 out of their last 9 head-to-head games, showcasing their ability to start strong against this opponent.

sections.subtitle: Inter's Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Inter boast several standout players in their lineup, including Hakan Çalhanoğlu, who holds the 1st league rank for average rating at 7.42, and Federico Dimarco, who leads the league with 16 big chances created. Up front, Lautaro Martínez, ranked 5th in the league for goals with 12, will be crucial despite underperforming his expected goals of 13.46. However, Inter are missing key forward Marcus Thuram, who has 14 goals and ranks 3rd in the league, alongside midfielder Denzel Dumfries with 6 goals. These absences could impact their attacking dynamism.

sections.subtitle: Roma's Key Contributors and Challenges
sections.text: Roma will rely on forward Artem Dovbyk, also ranked 5th in the league for goals with 12, to lead their attack, supported by Alexis Saelemaekers, who has overperformed his expected goals by 4.1085, scoring 6 goals. Matías Soulé, with a rating of 7.16, adds creativity upfront. However, Roma face a significant blow with the absence of Paulo Dybala, who holds a 2nd league rank for average rating at 7.39 and is fouled 2.5 times per game on average, ranking 5th in the league. His absence could dent Roma’s attacking flair.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Inter are currently on a streak of no wins in their last 3 matches, while also failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 8 games, which might give Roma some confidence. Roma, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last 5 matches, though their games have consistently seen under 2.5 goals in the last 5 outings. Inter’s home games often see more than 10.5 corners in 4 of their last 5 matches, hinting at a potentially open game with plenty of attacking intent.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Dominance by Inter
sections.text: Inter lead the league in several key stats, including total goals scored with 73, headers scored with 14, and big chances created with 114, all ranking 1st. They also dominate possession with an average of 59.89%, the highest in the league, and excel in penalty area entries per game at 31.66, again ranking 1st. Their aerial duels won percentage of 58.68% is also the best in the league, which could pose a challenge for Roma in set-piece situations.

sections.subtitle: Roma's Goal Distribution and Defensive Concerns
sections.text: Roma’s goal distribution shows they are most prolific between the 31st and 45th minutes, scoring 28.13% of their goals in that period, while they also net frequently late in games between the 61st and 75th minutes with 21.88% of their goals. However, Roma have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 11 head-to-head matches against Inter, a worrying trend as they face a side with Inter’s attacking prowess.

sections.subtitle: Notable Performers from Recent Encounters
sections.text: In the last 5 matches against Roma, Inter’s standout player has been goalkeeper Josep Martínez, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.57 and averaging 3.67 saves per game across 3 starts. For Roma, Mile Svilar has been in form over the last 5 matches with an average rating of 7.9, which could be vital in keeping Inter’s potent attack at bay.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Disciplinary Trends
sections.text: Inter’s high pressing is evident with 4.03 possessions won in the final third per game, ranking 2nd in the league, which could trouble Roma’s defense. Roma’s recent matches have seen fewer than 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 games, while head-to-head clashes between these teams have seen over 4.5 cards in 4 of the last 5 encounters, aligning with referee Michael Fabbri’s average of 4.8 yellows per game. Inter’s low yellow card count of 1.4 per game, ranking 2nd for the least in the league, contrasts with the potential for a heated battle.
